children.text: App can not discover my LG TV.

1. Be sure that your TV is supported.
This app only works with LG webOS TVs.

2. Check that your TV and Android device are in the same Wi-Fi network.

3. Ensure that your TV's 'LG Connect Apps' option is turned on. (Settings -> Network -> LG Connect Apps)

If you have verified all above cases, but still have no luck with getting your TV discovered, chances are, that your Wi-Fi hotspot does not have or has disabled SSDP support.

In that case you can try using any other device, that can function as a Wi-Fi hotspot and connect both your Android device and TV to it.
Nowadays, many smartphones have Wi-Fi hotspot built-in.

I start slideshow/video, but there is 
"Network Error" on my LG TV.
Check that your TV does have Internet connection.

I start video, but there is 
"Unable to play. The file cannot be recognized." - error on my LG TV.

Looks like the format of this video is not supported by LG webOS platform.

At the moment, LG webOS supports next video formats:

MP4(.mp4) with H.264/AVC codecs
ASF(.wmv, .asf) with VC-1 codec
TS(.ts) with H.264 codec
I start video, but there is only black screen on my LG TV.

Starting video playback may take some time to load on the TV, especially if TV was turned on a short time ago.

If black screen persists for a long while, then it is most likely the same issue as previous one.
I start slideshow, but all I see is constantly spinning 100% progress bar on my Android device and a black screen on my LG TV.

Launching slideshow may take some time to load on the TV, especially if TV was turned on recently.

It also may take a while, depending on your Internet connection speed.

Unfortunately, LG webOS platform does not support video rotation inside web apps.

Alternatively, you can try to rotate and save video on your Android device.
